flag.registry: Path to repos.yaml (auto-detected if not specified)

claude.long:
Tools for working with Claude Code.

Commands:
  run       Run Claude in the current directory
  config    Manage Claude configuration

run.short: Run Claude Code in the current directory

ai.long:
Manage tasks from the core-agentic service for AI-assisted development.

Commands:
  tasks          List tasks (filterable by status, priority, labels)
  task           View task details or auto-select highest priority
  task:update    Update task status or progress
  task:complete  Mark task as completed or failed
  task:commit    Create git commit with task reference
  task:pr        Create GitHub PR linked to task
  claude         Claude Code integration

Workflow:
  core ai tasks                      # List pending tasks
  core ai task --auto --claim        # Auto-select and claim a task
  core ai task:commit <id> -m 'msg'  # Commit with task reference
  core ai task:complete <id>         # Mark task done

flag.claim: Claim the task after showing details
flag.context: Show gathered context for AI collaboration

task.long:
Shows details of a specific task or auto-selects the highest priority task.

Examples:
  core ai task abc123           # Show task details
  core ai task abc123 --claim   # Show and claim the task
  core ai task abc123 --context # Show task with gathered context
  core ai task --auto           # Auto-select highest priority pending task
task.short: Show task details or auto-select a task

flag.scope: Scope for the commit type (e.g., auth, api, ui)

task_commit.long:
Creates a git commit with a task reference and co-author attribution.

Commit message format:
  feat(scope): description

  Task: #123
  Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

Examples:
  core ai task:commit abc123 --message 'add user authentication'
  core ai task:commit abc123 -m 'fix login bug' --scope auth
  core ai task:commit abc123 -m 'update docs' --push

task_complete.long:
Marks a task as completed with optional output and artifacts.

Examples:
  core ai task:complete abc123 --output 'Feature implemented'
  core ai task:complete abc123 --failed --error 'Build failed'

task_pr.branch_error: cannot create PR from {{.Branch}} branch; create a feature branch first
task_pr.long:
Creates a GitHub pull request linked to a task.

Requires the GitHub CLI (gh) to be installed and authenticated.

Examples:
  core ai task:pr abc123
  core ai task:pr abc123 --title 'Add authentication feature'
  core ai task:pr abc123 --draft --labels 'enhancement,needs-review'
  core ai task:pr abc123 --base develop
task_pr.short: Create a pull request for a task

task_update.flag_required: at least one of --status, --progress, or --notes required
task_update.long:
Updates a task's status, progress, or adds notes.

Examples:
  core ai task:update abc123 --status in_progress
  core ai task:update abc123 --progress 50 --notes 'Halfway done'

flag.limit: Max number of tasks to return
flag.priority: Filter by priority (critical, high, medium, low)
flag.status: Filter by status (pending, in_progress, completed, blocked)

tasks.hint: Use 'core ai task <id>' to view details
tasks.long:
Lists tasks from the core-agentic service.

Configuration is loaded from:
  1. Environment variables (AGENTIC_TOKEN, AGENTIC_BASE_URL)
  2. .env file in current directory
  3. ~/.core/agentic.yaml

Examples:
  core ai tasks
  core ai tasks --status pending --priority high
  core ai tasks --labels bug,urgent

error.invalid_target: invalid target format "{{.Target}}", expected OS/arch (e.g., linux/amd64)
error.no_project_type:
no supported project type detected in {{.Dir}}
Supported types: go (go.mod), wails (wails.json), node (package.json), php (composer.json)

flag.archive: Create archives (tar.gz for linux/darwin, zip for windows)
flag.ci: CI mode - minimal output with JSON artifact list at the end
flag.config: Config file path (for linuxkit: YAML config, for docker: Dockerfile)
flag.format: Output format for linuxkit (iso-bios, qcow2-bios, raw, vmdk)
flag.type: Builder type (go, wails, docker, linuxkit, taskfile) - auto-detected if not specified

error.must_be_directory: path specified must be a directory

flag.path: The path to the static web application files

build.long:
Builds the current project with automatic type detection.
Supports Go, Wails, Docker, LinuxKit, and Taskfile projects.
Configuration can be provided via .core/build.yaml or command-line flags.

Examples:
  core build                              # Auto-detect and build
  core build --type docker                # Build Docker image
  core build --type linuxkit              # Build LinuxKit image
  core build --type linuxkit --config linuxkit.yml --format qcow2-bios

flag.url: The URL of the PWA to build

pwa.no_manifest: no manifest file found. Proceeding with basic site download.
pwa.short: Build from a live PWA URL

flag.dry_run: Show what would be generated without writing files
flag.lang: Generate only this language (typescript, python, go, php)
flag.version: Version to embed in generated SDKs

sdk.label: Build SDK:
sdk.long:
Generates typed API clients from OpenAPI specifications.
Supports TypeScript, Python, Go, and PHP.

Examples:
  core build sdk                    # Generate all configured SDKs
  core build sdk --lang typescript  # Generate only TypeScript SDK
  core build sdk --spec api.yaml    # Use specific OpenAPI spec
sdk.short: Generate API SDKs from OpenAPI spec

build.short: Build projects with auto-detection and cross-compilation

changelog.long: Generates a changelog from conventional commits.

flag.go_for_launch: Actually publish (default is dry-run for safety)

ci.long:
Publishes pre-built artifacts from dist/ to configured targets.
Run 'core build' first to create artifacts.

SAFE BY DEFAULT: Runs in dry-run mode unless --we-are-go-for-launch is specified.

Configuration: .core/release.yaml

version.long: Shows the determined version or validates a version string.

ci.long:
Fetches GitHub Actions workflow status for all repos.
Shows latest run status for each repo.
Requires the 'gh' CLI to be installed and authenticated.
ci.short: Check CI status across all repos

flag.all: Commit all dirty repos without prompting

commit.long:
Uses Claude to create commits for dirty repos.
Shows uncommitted changes and invokes Claude to generate commit messages.

health.long:
Shows a summary of repository health:
total repos, dirty repos, unpushed commits, etc.
health.short: Quick health check across all repos

impact.long:
Analyzes the dependency graph to show which repos
would be affected by changes to the specified repo.
impact.requires_registry: impact analysis requires repos.yaml with dependency information
impact.short: Show impact of changing a repo

issues.long:
Fetches open issues from GitHub for all repos in the registry.
Requires the 'gh' CLI to be installed and authenticated.
issues.short: List open issues across all repos

dev.long:
Manage multiple git repositories and GitHub integration.

Uses repos.yaml to discover repositories. Falls back to scanning
the current directory if no registry is found.

Git Operations:
  work      Combined status -> commit -> push workflow
  health    Quick repo health summary
  commit    Claude-assisted commit messages
  push      Push repos with unpushed commits
  pull      Pull repos behind remote

GitHub Integration (requires gh CLI):
  issues    List open issues across repos
  reviews   List PRs awaiting review
  ci        Check GitHub Actions status
  impact    Analyse dependency impact

Dev Environment:
  install   Download dev environment image
  boot      Start dev environment VM
  stop      Stop dev environment VM
  shell     Open shell in dev VM
  status    Check dev VM status

pull.all_up_to_date: All repos up to date. Nothing to pull.

flag.all: Pull all repos, not just those behind

pull.long:
Pulls updates for all repos.
By default only pulls repos that are behind. Use --all to pull all repos.

push.all_up_to_date: All repos up to date. Nothing to push.
push.diverged_help: Some repos have diverged (local and remote have different commits).
push.long:
Pushes unpushed commits for all repos.
Shows repos with commits to push and confirms before pushing.
push.uncommitted_changes_commit: You have uncommitted changes. Commit with Claude first?

reviews.long:
Fetches open PRs from GitHub for all repos in the registry.
Shows review status (approved, changes requested, pending).
Requires the 'gh' CLI to be installed and authenticated.
reviews.short: List PRs needing review across all repos

sync.long:
This command scans the 'pkg' directory for services and ensures that the
top-level public API for each service is in sync with its internal implementation.
It automatically generates the necessary Go files with type aliases.
sync.short: Synchronizes public service APIs with internal implementations

boot.long:
Boots the dev environment VM.

Examples:
  core dev boot
  core dev boot --memory 8192 --cpus 4
  core dev boot --fresh

claude.long:
Starts a Claude Code session inside the dev environment sandbox.

Provides isolation while forwarding selected credentials.
Auto-boots the dev environment if not running.

Auth options (default: all):
  gh        - GitHub CLI auth
  anthropic - Anthropic API key
  ssh       - SSH agent forwarding
  git       - Git config (name, email)

Examples:
  core dev claude
  core dev claude --model opus
  core dev claude --auth gh,anthropic
  core dev claude --no-auth

install.long:
Downloads the platform-specific dev environment image.

The image includes Go, PHP, Node.js, Python, Docker, and Claude CLI.
Downloads are cached at ~/.core/images/

Examples:
  core dev install
install.short: Download and install the dev environment image

vm.not_installed: dev environment not installed (run 'core dev install' first)

serve.long:
Mounts the current project into the dev environment and starts a dev server.

Auto-detects the appropriate serve command based on project files.

Examples:
  core dev serve
  core dev serve --port 3000
  core dev serve --path public
serve.short: Mount project and start dev server

flag.console: Use serial console instead of SSH

shell.long:
Opens an interactive shell in the dev environment.

Uses SSH by default, or serial console with --console.

Examples:
  core dev shell
  core dev shell --console
  core dev shell -- ls -la

status.long:
Shows the current status of the dev environment.

Examples:
  core dev vm-status

stop.long:
Stops the running dev environment VM.

Examples:
  core dev stop

test.long:
Runs tests in the dev environment.

Auto-detects the test command based on project files, or uses .core/test.yaml.

Examples:
  core dev test
  core dev test --name integration
  core dev test -- go test -v ./...
test.short: Run tests in the dev environment

update.long:
Checks for dev environment updates and optionally applies them.

Examples:
  core dev update
  core dev update --apply

flag.commit: Use Claude to commit dirty repos before pushing

work.long:
Manage git status, commits, and pushes across multiple repositories.

Reads repos.yaml to discover repositories and their relationships.
Shows status, optionally commits with Claude, and pushes changes.
work.use_commit_flag: Use --commit to have Claude create commits

list.long:
List documentation files for all repos in the workspace.

Shows README.md, CLAUDE.md, CHANGELOG.md, and docs/ directory status
for each repository.

docs.long:
Manage documentation across all repos.
Scan for docs, check coverage, and sync to core-php/docs/packages/.

flag.dry_run: Show what would be synced without copying

sync.long:
Sync documentation from all repos to a central location.

Copies docs/ directories from each package to core-php/docs/packages/
for unified documentation builds.
sync.no_docs_found: No documentation found in any repos.

doctor.cli_auth_missing: CLI authentication - run: gh auth login
doctor.install_linux_header: # Install via your package manager or:
doctor.install_macos: brew install git gh php composer node pnpm docker
doctor.long:
Checks that all required tools are installed and configured.
Run this before 'core setup' to ensure your environment is ready.
doctor.no_repos_yaml: No repos.yaml found (run from workspace directory)
doctor.ssh_missing: SSH key missing - run: ssh-keygen && gh ssh-key add

flag.open: Generate and open HTML report in browser

cov.long:
Run tests and generate coverage report.

Examples:
  core go cov                  # Run with coverage summary
  core go cov --html           # Generate HTML report
  core go cov --open           # Generate and open HTML report
  core go cov --threshold 80   # Fail if coverage < 80%

flag.check: Check only, exit 1 if not formatted

fmt.long:
Format Go code using gofmt or goimports.

Examples:
  core go fmt              # Check formatting
  core go fmt --fix        # Fix formatting
  core go fmt --diff       # Show diff

install.long:
Install Go binary to $GOPATH/bin.

Examples:
  core go install              # Install current module
  core go install ./cmd/core   # Install specific path
  core go install --no-cgo     # Pure Go (no C dependencies)
  core go install -v           # Verbose output

lint.long:
Run golangci-lint on the codebase.

Examples:
  core go lint
  core go lint --fix

go.long:
Go development tools with enhanced output and environment setup.

Commands:
  test     Run tests
  cov      Run tests with coverage report
  fmt      Format Go code
  lint     Run golangci-lint
  install  Install Go binary
  mod      Module management (tidy, download, verify)
  work     Workspace management

mod.long:
Go module management commands.

Commands:
  tidy      Add missing and remove unused modules
  download  Download modules to local cache
  verify    Verify dependencies
  graph     Print module dependency graph

test.long:
Run Go tests with coverage reporting.

Sets MACOSX_DEPLOYMENT_TARGET=26.0 to suppress linker warnings.
Filters noisy output and provides colour-coded coverage.

Examples:
  core go test
  core go test --coverage
  core go test --pkg ./pkg/crypt
  core go test --run TestHash

work.long:
Go workspace management commands.

Commands:
  sync    Sync go.work with modules
  init    Initialize go.work
  use     Add module to workspace

analyse.long:
Run PHPStan or Larastan static analysis.

Auto-detects Larastan if installed, otherwise uses PHPStan.
analyse.no_analyser: no static analyser found (install PHPStan: composer require phpstan/phpstan --dev)

audit.long:
Check PHP and JavaScript dependencies for known vulnerabilities.

Runs composer audit and npm audit (if package.json exists).

flag.format: LinuxKit output format: qcow2 (default), iso, raw, vmdk
flag.name: Image name (default: project directory name)

build.long:
Build a production-ready container image for the PHP project.

By default, builds a Docker image using FrankenPHP.
Use --type linuxkit to build a LinuxKit VM image instead.

flag.force: Force deployment even if no changes detected

deploy.long:
Deploy the PHP application to Coolify.

Requires configuration in .env:
  COOLIFY_URL=https://coolify.example.com
  COOLIFY_TOKEN=your-api-token
  COOLIFY_APP_ID=production-app-id
  COOLIFY_STAGING_APP_ID=staging-app-id (optional)
deploy.triggered: Deployment triggered. Use 'core php deploy:status' to check progress.

flag.id: Specific deployment ID to rollback to

deploy_rollback.long:
Rollback to a previous deployment.

If no deployment ID is specified, rolls back to the most recent
successful deployment.
deploy_rollback.triggered: Rollback triggered. Use 'core php deploy:status' to check progress.

deploy_status.long: Show the status of a deployment.

flag.domain: Domain for SSL certificate (default: from APP_URL or localhost)

dev.long:
Starts all detected Laravel services.

Auto-detects:
  - Vite (vite.config.js/ts)
  - Horizon (config/horizon.php)
  - Reverb (config/reverb.php)
  - Redis (from .env)
dev.press_ctrl_c: Press Ctrl+C to stop all services

error.critical_high_issues: critical or high severity issues found
error.not_laravel: not a Laravel project (missing artisan or laravel/framework)

fmt.long: Format PHP code using Laravel Pint.
fmt.no_formatter: no formatter found (install Laravel Pint: composer require laravel/pint --dev)
fmt.short: Format PHP code with Laravel Pint

infection.long:
Run Infection mutation testing to measure test suite quality.

Mutation testing modifies your code and checks if tests catch
the changes. High mutation score = high quality tests.

Warning: This can be slow on large codebases.

logs.long:
Stream logs from Laravel services.

Services: frankenphp, vite, horizon, reverb, redis

php.long:
Manage Laravel development environment with FrankenPHP.

Services orchestrated:
  - FrankenPHP/Octane (port 8000, HTTPS on 443)
  - Vite dev server (port 5173)
  - Laravel Horizon (queue workers)
  - Laravel Reverb (WebSocket, port 8080)
  - Redis (port 6379)

link.done: Packages linked. Run 'composer update' to install.
link.long:
Link local PHP packages for development.

Adds path repositories to composer.json with symlink enabled.
The package name is auto-detected from each path's composer.json.

list.long:
List all locally linked packages.

Shows package name, path, and version for each linked package.

packages.long:
Link and manage local PHP packages for development.

Similar to npm link, this adds path repositories to composer.json
for developing packages alongside your project.

unlink.done: Packages unlinked. Run 'composer update' to remove.
unlink.long:
Remove linked packages from composer.json.

Removes path repositories by package name.

update.long:
Run composer update for linked packages.

If no packages specified, updates all packages.

psalm.analysing_fixing: Analysing and fixing code with Psalm
psalm.long:
Run Psalm deep static analysis with Laravel plugin support.

Psalm provides deeper type inference than PHPStan and catches
different classes of bugs. Both should be run for best coverage.

qa.fix_infection: Improve test coverage for mutated code

flag.full: Run all stages including slow checks

qa.long:
Run the complete quality assurance pipeline.

Stages:
  quick:    Security audit, code style, PHPStan
  standard: Psalm, tests
  full:     Rector dry-run, mutation testing (slow)

rector.long:
Run Rector for automated code improvements and PHP upgrades.

Rector can automatically upgrade PHP syntax, improve code quality,
and apply framework-specific refactorings.

flag.sarif: Output in SARIF format (for GitHub Security)

security.long:
Scan for security vulnerabilities in configuration and code.

Checks environment config, file permissions, code patterns,
and runs security-focused static analysis.

serve.long:
Run a production PHP container.

This starts the built Docker image in production mode.
serve.name_required: --name is required: specify the Docker image name

shell.long: Open an interactive shell in a running PHP container.

flag.group: Run only tests in specified group

test.long:
Run PHP tests using PHPUnit or Pest.

Auto-detects Pest if tests/Pest.php exists, otherwise uses PHPUnit.

error.auth_failed: authentication failed - try: unset GH_TOKEN && gh auth login
error.gh_not_authenticated: gh CLI not authenticated. Run: gh auth login
error.invalid_repo_format: invalid repo format: use org/repo (e.g., host-uk/core-php)
error.no_repos_yaml_workspace: no repos.yaml found - run from workspace directory
error.repo_required: repository is required (e.g., core pkg install host-uk/core-php)

install.long:
Clones a repository from GitHub.

Examples:
  core pkg install host-uk/core-php
  core pkg install host-uk/core-tenant --dir ./packages
  core pkg install host-uk/core-admin --add

list.long:
Lists all packages in the current workspace.

Reads from repos.yaml or scans for git repositories.

Examples:
  core pkg list
list.title: Installed Packages

pkg.long:
Manage host-uk/core-* packages and repositories.

Commands:
  search    Search GitHub for packages
  install   Clone a package from GitHub
  list      List installed packages
  update    Update installed packages
  outdated  Check for outdated packages

outdated.long:
Checks which packages have unpulled commits.

Examples:
  core pkg outdated

flag.refresh: Bypass cache and fetch fresh data
flag.type: Filter by type in name (mod, services, plug, website)

search.gh_token_warning: GH_TOKEN env var is set - this may cause auth issues
search.long:
Searches GitHub for repositories matching a pattern.
Uses gh CLI for authenticated search. Results are cached for 1 hour.

Examples:
  core pkg search                           # List all host-uk repos
  core pkg search --pattern 'core-*'        # Search for core-* repos
  core pkg search --org mycompany           # Search different org
  core pkg search --refresh                 # Bypass cache

update.long:
Pulls latest changes for installed packages.

Examples:
  core pkg update core-php       # Update specific package
  core pkg update --all          # Update all packages

error.base_required: --base is required (version tag or file path)

diff.label: SDK Diff:
diff.long:
Check for breaking API changes between spec versions.

Compares a base spec (version tag or file) against the current spec
and reports any breaking changes that would affect API consumers.

sdk.long:
Tools for validating OpenAPI specs and checking API compatibility.
To generate SDKs, use: core build sdk

Commands:
  diff      Check for breaking API changes
  validate  Validate OpenAPI spec syntax
sdk.short: SDK validation and API compatibility tools

validate.long:
Validate OpenAPI spec syntax.

Checks that the OpenAPI specification file is valid and well-formed.

flag.dry_run: Show what would be cloned without cloning
flag.name: Project directory name for bootstrap mode
flag.only: Only clone repos of these types (comma-separated: foundation,module,product)
flag.registry: Path to repos.yaml (auto-detected if not specified)

setup.long:
Sets up a development workspace.

REGISTRY MODE (repos.yaml exists):
  Clones all repositories defined in repos.yaml into packages/.
  Skips repos that already exist. Use --only to filter by type.

BOOTSTRAP MODE (no repos.yaml):
  1. Clones core-devops to set up the workspace
  2. Presents an interactive wizard to select packages
  3. Clones selected packages

Use --all to skip the wizard and clone everything.
setup.short: Bootstrap workspace or clone packages from registry

wizard.git_repo_title: This directory is a git repository
wizard.project_name_desc: Enter the name for your new workspace directory
wizard.selection_hint: Use space to select/deselect, enter to confirm
wizard.what_to_do: What would you like to do?

error.no_go_mod: no go.mod found - run from a Go project directory

flag.run: Run only tests matching this regex (-run)
flag.verbose: Show test output as it runs (-v)

test.long:
Runs Go tests with coverage reporting.

Sets MACOSX_DEPLOYMENT_TARGET=26.0 to suppress linker warnings on macOS.

Examples:
  core test                     # Run all tests with coverage summary
  core test --verbose           # Show test output as it runs
  core test --coverage          # Show detailed per-package coverage
  core test --pkg ./pkg/...     # Test specific packages
  core test --run TestName      # Run specific test by name
  core test --short             # Skip long-running tests
  core test --race              # Enable race detector
  core test --json              # Output JSON for CI/agents

error.id_and_cmd_required: container ID and command are required
error.linuxkit_not_found: linuxkit not found. Install with: brew install linuxkit (macOS) or see https://github.com/linuxkit/linuxkit
error.multiple_match: multiple containers match '{{.ID}}', be more specific

exec.long:
Execute a command inside a running VM via SSH.

Examples:
  core vm exec abc12345 ls -la
  core vm exec abc1 /bin/sh
exec.short: Execute a command in a VM

hint.view_logs: Use 'core vm logs {{.ID}}' to view output

logs.long:
View logs from a VM.

Examples:
  core vm logs abc12345
  core vm logs -f abc1

vm.long:
Manage LinuxKit virtual machines.

LinuxKit VMs are lightweight, immutable VMs built from YAML templates.
They run using qemu or hyperkit depending on your system.

Commands:
  run        Run a VM from image or template
  ps         List running VMs
  stop       Stop a running VM
  logs       View VM logs
  exec       Execute command in VM
  templates  Manage LinuxKit templates

ps.header: ID	NAME	IMAGE	STATUS	STARTED	PID
ps.long:
Lists all VMs. By default, only shows running VMs.

Examples:
  core vm ps
  core vm ps -a

flag.name: Name for the container
flag.template: Run from a LinuxKit template (build + run)
flag.var: Template variable in KEY=VALUE format (can be repeated)

run.long:
Runs a LinuxKit image as a VM using the available hypervisor.

Supported image formats: .iso, .qcow2, .vmdk, .raw

You can also run from a template using --template, which will build and run
the image automatically. Use --var to set template variables.

Examples:
  core vm run image.iso
  core vm run -d image.qcow2
  core vm run --name myvm --memory 2048 --cpus 4 image.iso
  core vm run --template core-dev --var SSH_KEY="ssh-rsa AAAA..."
  core vm run --template server-php --var SSH_KEY="..." --var DOMAIN=example.com
run.short: Run a LinuxKit image or template

stop.long:
Stops a running VM by ID.

Examples:
  core vm stop abc12345
  core vm stop abc1

templates.long:
Manage LinuxKit YAML templates for building VMs.

Templates provide pre-configured LinuxKit configurations for common use cases.
They support variable substitution with ${VAR} and ${VAR:-default} syntax.

Examples:
  core vm templates                    # List available templates
  core vm templates show core-dev      # Show template content
  core vm templates vars server-php    # Show template variables

show.long:
Display the content of a LinuxKit template.

Examples:
  core templates show core-dev
  core templates show server-php

templates.title: Available LinuxKit Templates

vars.long:
Display all variables used in a template.

Shows required variables (no default) and optional variables (with defaults).

Examples:
  core templates vars core-dev
  core templates vars server-php

error.gh_not_found: 'gh' CLI not found. Install from https://cli.github.com/
